participants.name: A (466eox)
participants.professional_persona: A generative AI specialist who joined a local unicorn startup after completing an AI residency at a Big Tech company. She builds and fine-tunes large language models for customer support automation in the financial services sector.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in transformer architectures, prompt engineering, and model fine-tuning (LoRA, RLHF); experienced with GPU clusters and model deployment on cloud platforms (GCP, AWS).
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Desires to conduct foundational AI research in resource-efficient models and to help establish Singapore as a center of excellence for generative AI.

participants.name: Jeremy
participants.professional_persona: Jeremy Tan is a Research Manager at a local AI startup that develops fraud detection systems for banks. He holds a Master’s degree and is known for rapidly prototyping solutions that meet industry compliance standards.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep knowledge of anomaly detection, graph neural networks, and secure multi-party computation. Experienced in navigating financial regulations to deploy AI in banking environments.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to grow his startup into a regional leader in AI-driven financial security. Interested in shaping Singapore’s regulatory sandbox for AI in finance.

participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: He aims to lead a cross‑sector AI ethics committee and help shape industry guidelines that balance innovation with public safety.

participants.name: Mid-level

participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to become a lead AI architect within five years and help shape Singapore's fintech AI ecosystem. Wants to mentor junior practitioners through TeSA career conversion programmes.

The co-funding cap of S$330,000 per 100E project is clearly stated in the policy context and aligns with what I've seen in industry. For compute infrastructure, I estimate around 500,000 GPU hours available for AI experimentation in 2023, based on typical allocations for national AI programmes and the need to support both research and industry use cases. For trainers, I estimate about 50 AI experts and trainers, considering the specialized nature of AI mentorship and the scale of programmes like AIAP and 100E.

No disagreements emerged across the five participants.
